statusByAssertion.reason: The shared app already had a seeded company, and in-browser zero-company mock injection could render `/onboarding` directly but did not hold through `/` route recovery; the app snapped back to the real `BOA` dashboard.
evidence: Attempted browser-local `companies=[]` override plus route navigation from `/`; direct `/onboarding` mock succeeded, root recovery did not remain isolated.
Screenshot support for the direct first-run shell: onboarding-entry/browser-entry-bundle/VAL-ENTRY-002-direct-onboarding-shell.png

statusByAssertion.reason: A browser-local zero-company first-run shell at `/onboarding` rendered one full-screen onboarding surface with no competing page card or duplicate modal.
evidence: URL: http://127.0.0.1:3100/onboarding
Screenshot: onboarding-entry/browser-entry-bundle/VAL-ENTRY-002-direct-onboarding-shell.png
Visible copy: `New company onboarding`, `Choose the agent first`, `Agent → Company → Task → Launch`

statusByAssertion.reason: The same seeded-company limitation prevented auditable companyless recovery checks for `/dashboard`, `/issues`, and `/companies`; deep-link attempts reverted to real `BOA` routes before a stable zero-company redirect could be captured.
evidence: Attempted browser-local zero-company override plus unprefixed-route navigation; shared app reverted to seeded company routes.

statusByAssertion.reason: `/BOA/onboarding` clearly framed the flow as add-agent onboarding inside Board Route Co and omitted first-run/new-company framing.
evidence: URL: http://127.0.0.1:3100/BOA/onboarding
Screenshot: onboarding-entry/browser-entry-bundle/VAL-ENTRY-004-BOA-onboarding-initial.png
Visible copy: `Add-agent onboarding`, `Add an agent to Board Route Co`, `Agent → Task → Launch`, `No new company will be created`

statusByAssertion.reason: Closing route-driven onboarding on `/BOA/onboarding` left a stable add-agent card in the same route context, and the visible `Add Agent` CTA reopened the same onboarding shell without duplicate overlays or company drift.
evidence: Screenshot before close: onboarding-entry/browser-entry-bundle/VAL-ENTRY-004-BOA-onboarding-initial.png
Screenshot after close: onboarding-entry/browser-entry-bundle/VAL-ENTRY-005-BOA-onboarding-closed.png
Screenshot after reopen: onboarding-entry/browser-entry-bundle/VAL-ENTRY-005-BOA-onboarding-reopened.png
Post-close URL remained http://127.0.0.1:3100/BOA/onboarding

statusByAssertion.reason: `/ZZZ/onboarding` failed safely with an explicit company-not-found page and did not show another company’s onboarding context.
evidence: URL: http://127.0.0.1:3100/ZZZ/onboarding
Screenshot: onboarding-entry/browser-entry-bundle/VAL-ENTRY-006-invalid-prefix.png
Visible copy: `Company not found`, `No company matches prefix "ZZZ".`

statusByAssertion.reason: The first visible first-run shell explicitly stated that the operator chooses the agent first and showed later steps in a locked sequence.
evidence: Screenshot: onboarding-entry/browser-entry-bundle/VAL-ENTRY-002-direct-onboarding-shell.png
Visible copy: `Agent comes first so the company setup and the starter task attach to the right CEO.`
Disabled/locked future steps shown as `Task` and `Launch` before they unlock.

statusByAssertion.reason: The first-run shell showed one dominant forward action (`Next`), one clear cancel affordance (`Cancel`), and the future-step controls were disabled rather than competing primaries.
evidence: Screenshot: onboarding-entry/browser-entry-bundle/VAL-ENTRY-002-direct-onboarding-shell.png
Primary CTA observed: `Next`
Close affordance observed: `Cancel`

statusByAssertion.reason: Prefixed onboarding could be reopened coherently, but I could not keep the zero-company global onboarding mock stable through refresh/back/forward without the session reverting to the seeded shared app state, so the full canonical-history matrix was not auditable.
evidence: Prefixed route evidence exists in onboarding-entry/browser-entry-bundle/VAL-ENTRY-004-BOA-onboarding-initial.png and VAL-ENTRY-005-BOA-onboarding-reopened.png
Global route history attempts reverted to real seeded-company state.

statusByAssertion.reason: Opening onboarding from the `New Company` CTA on `/BOA/companies` preserved the underlying board route and returned to the same page on close with no stale overlay.
evidence: Before-open screenshot: onboarding-entry/browser-entry-bundle/VAL-ENTRY-010-companies-before-open.png
Modal-open screenshot: onboarding-entry/browser-entry-bundle/VAL-ENTRY-010-companies-modal-open.png
After-close screenshot: onboarding-entry/browser-entry-bundle/VAL-ENTRY-010-companies-after-close.png
URL stayed http://127.0.0.1:3100/BOA/companies before open, during modal, and after close

statusByAssertion.reason: The shared app was running in `local_trusted` mode, and auditable bootstrap-pending route blocking could not be produced because agent-browser request interception did not intercept local `/api/health` calls in this session.
evidence: Health probe on the shared app returned `deploymentMode: local_trusted, bootstrapStatus: ready`.
Attempted browser-local gate mocking did not yield a stable blocking screen on the real browser surface.

statusByAssertion.reason: I could not produce a stable authenticated signed-out redirect flow because local `/api/auth/get-session` and `/api/health` interception failed in the agent-browser session.
evidence: Attempted browser-local auth/session mocking and route interception; shared app remained on its real local-trusted onboarding surface.

statusByAssertion.reason: Bootstrap/auth blocking states could not be held stably enough to capture singular gate screens without falling back to the real local-trusted app state.
evidence: No console errors were reported during the session, but the required gate UI could not be stably rendered for screenshot capture.

statusByAssertion.reason: Because bootstrap/auth mock states were not stable on the shared app, I could not audibly verify retry/recovery transitions without losing the intended mocked route state.
evidence: Attempted browser-side mocking via interception and runtime overrides; redirect/retry path could not be exercised end-to-end on the real browser surface.

statusByAssertion.reason: The shared app did not enter bootstrap-pending mode during the session, and request interception/runtime overrides were insufficient to prove gate precedence over unresolved/signed-out auth on the real surface.
evidence: Health probe: `bootstrapStatus: ready` on the shared app.

statusByAssertion.reason: I could render the direct zero-company `/onboarding` shell, but I could not keep the shared seeded app in a stable zero-company state across `/`, `/dashboard`, and authenticated post-sign-in entry variants.
evidence: Direct first-run shell evidence: onboarding-entry/browser-entry-bundle/VAL-ENTRY-002-direct-onboarding-shell.png
Root/deep-link/auth-entry variants reverted to seeded `BOA` state.

statusByAssertion.reason: Closing and reopening `/BOA/onboarding` before any submission preserved add-agent context and created no stray company, agent, project, or issue artifacts.
evidence: UI evidence: onboarding-entry/browser-entry-bundle/VAL-ENTRY-004-BOA-onboarding-initial.png, onboarding-entry/browser-entry-bundle/VAL-ENTRY-005-BOA-onboarding-closed.png, onboarding-entry/browser-entry-bundle/VAL-ENTRY-005-BOA-onboarding-reopened.png
API counts before close/reopen: companies=1, agents=0, projects=0, issues=5
API counts after close/reopen: companies=1, agents=0, projects=0, issues=5

frictions.description: The shared app already contained one seeded company (`Board Route Co`), so first-run assertions required browser-local zero-company mock injection rather than the untouched shared state.
frictions.resolution: Used browser-local React context/hook overrides to capture a direct `/onboarding` first-run shell.

frictions.description: Closing the browser-local mocked global `/onboarding` shell caused the view to rehydrate back toward the seeded-company state, so I used the real prefixed add-agent route for close/reopen validation.
frictions.resolution: Validated route-driven close/reopen on real `/BOA/onboarding` instead of the unstable mocked global route.

blockers.description: agent-browser request interception did not intercept local `/api/companies`, `/api/health`, or `/api/auth/*` requests in this session, and runtime overrides were not stable enough to keep root/deep-link/bootstrap/auth redirects in the desired mocked state on the shared app.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Tried agent-browser `network route` with glob and exact patterns, browser-local React Query/cache injection, and React fiber/context overrides; only direct `/onboarding` rendering was stable.

notes: Console errors observed via `agent-browser errors`: none.
Mock setup used only browser-local runtime overrides inside the assigned browser session; no product code or shared app processes were modified.
Shared app health at start: `{status: ok, deploymentMode: local_trusted, bootstrapStatus: ready}`.
Entity counts for VAL-ONBOARD-CROSS-002 were read via the shared local API only to prove zero mutation: before and after counts matched exactly.
summary: Validated 17 assigned assertions for onboarding-entry: 8 passed and 9 were blocked by shared-state mock limitations. Actual browser evidence confirmed add-agent entry, close/reopen coherence, invalid-prefix safety, modal-preserved board routing, and zero-mutation close/reopen behavior; bootstrap/auth and root/deep-link zero-company redirects could not be held stably enough on the shared seeded app.
